basics.name: William J. Cosgrove
basics.label: Curriculum Designer, Educator, and Researcher
basics.summary: Curriculum designer and learning systems thinker with graduate-level training in instructional design, AI-integrated pedagogy, and equity-centered education. Experienced in designing onboarding and training content, translating complex research into actionable learning experiences, and managing cross-functional projects in fast-paced environments. Skilled in prompt engineering, data-driven insight delivery, and building scalable educational frameworks from the ground up.

education.area: Curriculum Design and Pedagogy, Concentration in Interdisciplinary Studies
courses: EDUC 6402 Trends and Issues in Emerging Technologies

work.name: Cue Insights
work.summary: Market research firm specializing in healthcare and pharmaceutical research.
highlights: Synthesize complex qualitative and quantitative research into strategic recommendations for healthcare and pharmaceutical clients
Designed and built QGen, a company-wide project management and survey analysis platform
Developed AI-driven qualitative coding tools replacing manual coding processes
Present research findings directly to senior client stakeholders
Evaluate survey instruments for measurement validity and reliability

work.name: George Washington University
highlights: Designing original metacognitive curriculum framework (Axiomatica) synthesizing Dewey, Bruner, Fink, Pinar, and schema theory
Partnered with GW Open-Source Program Office to design gamified U.S. history simulation
Created self-paced instructional module for AI-assisted lesson planning
Developed AI in Civics curriculum for secondary students

work.name: Bethesda-Chevy Chase High School (MCPS)
work.position: Student Teacher, AP US History and AP Government
highlights: Designed and independently led instruction for five AP sections
Constructed original AP Government curriculum around democratic institutions and federalism
Adapted curriculum for IEP, 504, and ESL learners
Navigated multi-stakeholder dynamics in IEP and 504 processes

work.name: Swim Safe
highlights: Co-founded and scaled community swim safety school serving 500+ students
Designed individualized adaptive curriculum for diverse learners
Managed all operations, hiring, and financial oversight across multiple locations

certificates.name: DC Educator Credential
certificates.issuer: OSSE (Office of the State Superintendent of Education)

certificates.name: Graduate Certificate in Curriculum Writing, Evaluation, and Development
Praxis: Principles of Learning and Teaching

skills.name: Technology and AI
keywords: Local AI Agents (n8n, LLMs, vector memory retrieval)

volunteer.summary: Weekly food distribution across underserved neighborhoods and schools in the DMV
20+ hours/week training. Special Olympics coaching, youth swim instruction, and service projects.
